message.content:
Thought for 3s

Orchid care (especially for common Phalaenopsis/moth orchids) focuses on mimicking their epiphytic nature: good air flow to roots, bright indirect light, and careful watering. Most die from overwatering‚ÄĒroots need to dry somewhat between waterings.‚Ā†Aos

1. Light (Key for Blooming)
Provide bright, indirect light ‚ÄĒ east or west window ideal; shaded south window works. Avoid direct midday sun (burns leaves) or deep shade (no blooms).‚Ā†Usbg
Leaves should be medium green. Too dark = dark green, leggy growth, no flowers. Too bright = yellowish/reddish leaves.
Supplement with grow lights if needed in low-light homes.
2. Watering (Biggest Killer)
Water thoroughly when the potting mix feels dry (usually once a week; more in heat/dry air, less in cool/humid). Lift the pot‚ÄĒif light, water.‚Ā†Vivier
Method: Place in sink, drench with room-temperature water for 5‚Äď10 minutes, let drain completely. Never let it sit in water. Water in the morning so foliage dries by evening.
Use bark mix (not soil). Ice cubes are not recommended‚ÄĒcold can damage roots.‚Ā†Discoverandshare
3. Temperature & Humidity
Day: 70‚Äď80¬įF (21‚Äď27¬įC). Night: 60‚Äď70¬įF (16‚Äď21¬įC). A 10¬įF nighttime drop helps trigger blooming.‚Ā†Westerlay
Humidity: 40‚Äď70%. Use a pebble tray with water (pot not sitting in it), humidifier, or group plants together. Good air circulation prevents rot.
4. Fertilizer
Use a balanced orchid fertilizer (e.g., 20-20-20) at half strength every 1‚Äď2 weeks during growth (spring/summer). Reduce in winter or when resting.‚Ā†Almanac
Water first, then fertilize. Flush with plain water monthly to prevent salt buildup.
5. Blooming & Aftercare
After flowers fade: Cut the spike 1 inch above a node (or at base if weak). Move to slightly brighter/warmer spot and continue care‚ÄĒnew spikes often form in 2‚Äď6 months with good light + temp drop.‚Ā†Calcoastorchids
Repot every 1‚Äď2 years after blooming (or when mix breaks down/roots crowded) into fresh orchid bark. Trim dead roots.
Quick Troubleshooting
Yellow leaves/bottom leaves dropping: Normal aging or overwatering.
Black/soft roots: Rot from overwatering‚ÄĒtrim bad roots, repot in fresh mix.
No blooms: Increase light or add cooler nights.
Wrinkled leaves: Underwatering.

Start simple: Bright indirect light + weekly thorough watering with full drainage. Orchids are resilient once you get the rhythm‚ÄĒmany grocery store ones rebloom for years. Adjust based on your specific variety and home conditions if it differs from Phalaenopsis.
